ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ joŋ gɛrɩ-la bil ʋ teeŋ ʋ bala kɔ.
translated: The dog chases the cat up the tree and it climbs.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Lee-la nɛ ʋ laa sɩpaaŋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a yɩrɛ La TƖƖNA yɩrɩŋ.
translated: The woman was going to spend the night at Willsville because of La Tɛɛna.

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bɩra pɩɛsɛba, “Ʋ yaraa fɩɛlaa?” Ba bʋl, “Ʋ yaraa fɩɛla nɛ. Na ʋ tolo Rakɛl nɛ kɛŋ ʋ piesee kʋkɔ ŋɩɩ.”
translated: She said to her husband, “Where are you going?” He replied, “I am going to where I was born. I will go and see my mother there.”

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Abɩraham sʋʋsa yi La TƖƖNA a pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ jaŋ kpʋ tʋɔpʋl tɩŋŋaa arɩ nɩbɔŋŋɔɔ kala hɛ dɔŋɔɔ?
translated: Does Abraham really think that La TƖƖNA will say, “Why should I help these people, are they not my own?”

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa laa Liya sʋla ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl ʋ hambolibii-nɔŋ a pɩ Jekɔb.
translated: The Lord said to Jacob, "Return to the land of your ancestors and to your native kin; I will be with you."
